text:
Market today: Chip stocks are getting hit hard - Samsung and SK Hynix both dropped 9%+ overnight, and that rout is spreading. That's the real driver today. Your sector (tech/semis) is under direct pressure, not just general market noise.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the one demanding attention - it's a 3x leveraged ETF already deep in the red, and your stop at $200 is close to current price ($217.55); a continued chip selloff could trigger it fast. SPAL (-13.72%) is brand-new, very volatile, and already near its tight stop at $21.38 (price $21.70) - margin amplifies both of these badly if they break. SMH (-2.5%) is softer but holding above its $540 stop for now.

Watch: Whether the Samsung/SK Hynix news drags TSM, SMH, and SOXL lower at the open. Also watch if SOXL breaks $200 - that's your hard line.

Bottom line: Protect capital today - the chip rout is live and your most leveraged names are in the crosshairs.
plays: SOXL ($217.55, -14.52%): Respect the $200 stop hard - chip rout spreading globally; if it breaks, don't hesitate. Consider trimming now given margin exposure.
SPAL ($21.70, -13.72%): Stop at $21.38 is razor-thin from current price. New leveraged position on margin - be ready to cut immediately if it touches that level.
No new buys today - chip sector under active selling pressure; adding exposure here means catching a falling knife on margin.
